div.head: The Chairman of the American Delegation (Hull) to the Acting Secretary of State Transmitted to President Roosevelt, aboard U. S. S. Ellis, as telegram No. 5, June 20, 6 p.m.
div.p:
47. For the President from Warburg. Discussion in second committee of Monetary Commission was centered entirely on our resolutionSee fourth resolution in “Memorandum on Policy for American Delegation,” pp. 622, 626; for discussion, see Journal of the Monetary and Economic Conference, pp. 66–67. introduced yesterday. At today’s session committee unanimously accepted sections (a) and (b) and referred remainder of first half to subcommittee appointed for working out technical details of an eventual permanent gold standard and referred second half of resolution relating to silver to second subcommittee under chairmanship of Pittman. This result highly gratifying and precisely what
we were working for. First committee has so far debated resolution introduced by ChamberlainNeville Chamberlain, British Chancelor of the Exchequer. on immediate monetary and credit policy which closely resembles first half of our corresponding resolution. We have not joined debate since through Cox chairmanship we can get in our work more easily in drafting committee which will probably be appointed tomorrow. [Warburg.]
